text  
the number of ways to arrange three indistinguishable rooks on a 6×6 board such that no two rooks are attacking each other is calculated by first choosing 3 rows and 3 columns and then placing the rooks in those rows and columns.  
the first step is to choose 3 rows out of 6, which can be done in 6 choose 3 ways.  
the second step is to choose 3 columns out of 6, which can be done in 6 choose 3 ways.  
the final step is to place the rooks in the selected rows and columns, ensuring that no two are in the same row or column.  
this results in a total of 6 choose 3 multiplied by 6 choose 3 multiplied by 3! ways to arrange the rooks.  
the total number of ways is therefore 20 × 20 × 6 = 2400.  
piece  
the final answer is 2400.
